text:
Once upon a quiet town called Willow Creek, there lived a girl named Mia. She loved adventure more than anything—even collecting antique birds nests. One afternoon, curiosity got the best of her. She stumbled upon an old weathered book buried underneath an old oak tree near her favorite owl's nest.

The book was titled *"The Wanderlings"*.

Excitedly, she read about forgotten voyages across the Atlantic. Overnight she became obsessed with thinking about journeys she'd never actually take. She talked nightly of sails and foggy ports.

Then one night she dreamed a dream; she sailed past islands unknown. Next morning she put on her old sailor clothes.

She discovered tiny villages along cliffs hanging over seas—younger than anything she knew.

She lived there for months, gathering stories under starlit skies.

When autumn came, she left for home, bringing whispers of songs birds sing. That year, an owl perched like her favorite book feather.

She returned eager for tales—not hers.

She never left
